verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (kallikrein-related peptidase 2, human glandular kallikrein hK2) is a secreted serine protease produced by prostate epithelium, released into seminal fluid and detectable in serum. It is synthesized with a signal peptide and prepro-region, processed in the secretory pathway, and released constitutively as a soluble enzyme — no TM domain, no GPI anchor, no lipidation. Contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented cancer/cell-death/activation ecto-pool of the protein body; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — prostate-restricted expression, but the protein is secreted, not surface-anchored, in that lineage; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented PM minority pool or TM isoform; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no wash-resistant covalent or non-covalent tethering to a TM partner documented; clinical PSMA-like surface programs do not exist for KLK2 itself (anti-KLK2 efforts target the soluble enzyme or pMHC).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Some radioligand/antibody efforts (e.g., h11B6) reportedly bind KLK2 associated with prostate cell surfaces; unclear if this represents wash-resistant tethering vs reversible enzyme-inhibitor capture.
